name: Monolithic Application
description: A traditional monolithic application architecture where all business logic resides in a single deployable unit. The application handles HTTP requests through a web server, processes them in an integrated application layer, and persists data to a shared database. Common in established enterprise systems and smaller-scale applications.

zones.name: external
zones.description: All external actors and systems accessing the application from outside the organizational perimeter, including end users via browsers and external API consumers.

zones.name: dmz
zones.description: The web-facing layer that handles TLS termination, static content serving, and initial request filtering before forwarding to the application server.

zones.name: application-tier
zones.description: The monolithic application server containing all business logic, authentication, authorization, and request processing. Runs as a single process or clustered instances behind the reverse proxy.

zones.name: data-tier
zones.description: The data persistence layer containing the primary database and any file storage. All access is mediated through the application tier using the ORM or data access layer.

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph external["External (Untrusted)"]
        user[End User / Browser]
        api_consumer[API Consumer]
    end
    subgraph dmz["DMZ (Semi-Trusted)"]
        waf[WAF]
        proxy(Reverse Proxy / Web Server)
    end
    subgraph app["Application Tier (Trusted)"]
        monolith(Monolithic Application Server)
        sessions[(Session Store)]
        jobs(Background Job Processor)
    end
    subgraph data["Data Tier (Highly Trusted)"]
        db[(Primary Database)]
        fs[(File Storage)]
        backup[(Backup Storage)]
    end
    user -->|HTTPS| waf
    api_consumer -->|HTTPS| waf
    waf --> proxy
    proxy -->|Session Auth| monolith
    monolith --> sessions
    monolith --> jobs
    monolith -->|ORM / SQL| db
    monolith -->|File I/O| fs
    db -->|Scheduled| backup
